All Week Long!.name: Days of the Week Calendar
All Week Long!.overview: Students create a visual calendar to reinforce their understanding of the days of the week. They mark and label each day, including weekdays and weekends, and illustrate common daily activities.
All Week Long!.general_objective: Develop time awareness and calendar skills while practicing days of the week vocabulary.
specific_objectives: Identify and name the seven days of the week
Associate daily activities with specific days
Create a visual representation of a weekly schedule
activities: Introduce days of the week through songs and chants
Draw and label activities for each day
Present calendars to classmates explaining their weekly schedule
All Week Long!.technology_integration: Use an online calendar tool to fill in days and activities.
All Week Long!.differentiation: Provide pre-printed day labels for struggling students; allow choice of digital or physical format.

All Week Long!.name: Day of the Week Song
All Week Long!.overview: Students create and sing a song about the days of the week using simple sentences like 'Today is Monday.'
All Week Long!.general_objective: Reinforce days of the week vocabulary through musical expression and repetition.
specific_objectives: Memorize the sequence of days through song
Use correct pronunciation of day names
Incorporate simple sentences in musical format
activities: Brainstorm new lyrics as a class
Record or perform the song for others
All Week Long!.technology_integration: Record the song using a tablet or computer; create a simple music video.
All Week Long!.differentiation: Provide visual prompts for lyrics; allow students to hum or clap instead of singing full lyrics if needed.

All Week Long!.name: Daily Schedule Chart
All Week Long!.overview: Students create a chart outlining their activities each day of the week, labeling each activity with simple sentences like 'On Monday, I play.'
All Week Long!.general_objective: Develop planning skills and associate language with daily routines.
activities: Draw and label activities for each day
All Week Long!.technology_integration: Use simple scheduling apps or create digital charts with teacher assistance.
All Week Long!.differentiation: Provide picture cards of common activities; use sentence frames for writing.

Nice Weather Today!.name: Weather Wheel
Nice Weather Today!.overview: Children create a 'weather wheel' with pictures of sunny, rainy, and windy weather and use it to describe the daily weather in class.
Nice Weather Today!.general_objective: Develop observation skills and weather-related vocabulary through hands-on creation.
specific_objectives: Identify basic weather conditions (sunny, rainy, windy)
Use weather vocabulary in daily descriptions
Observe and record daily weather patterns
activities: Create weather wheel with movable arrow
Practice using wheel to show current weather

rubric_criteria.Usage: Regular use for daily weather reporting

Nice Weather Today!.technology_integration: Use an interactive weather app to learn about different types of weather.
Nice Weather Today!.differentiation: Pre-cut weather symbols; provide verbal prompts for descriptions.

Nice Weather Today!.name: Weather Journal
Nice Weather Today!.overview: Students maintain a weather journal where they draw the day's weather and write sentences like 'It is sunny.'
Nice Weather Today!.general_objective: Develop consistent documentation habits and written expression skills.
Nice Weather Today!.technology_integration: Use a digital journaling app with weather icons.
Nice Weather Today!.differentiation: Provide sentence starters; allow drawing-only entries initially.

Nice Weather Today!.name: Weather Report Video
Nice Weather Today!.overview: Students act as weather reporters and record short videos describing the weather using sentences like 'It is raining today.'
Nice Weather Today!.general_objective: Develop presentation skills and confidence in spoken English through role-play.
specific_objectives: Use appropriate reporter language and posture
Create simple visual aids for report
Nice Weather Today!.technology_integration: Use tablet or phone for recording; simple editing if possible.
Nice Weather Today!.differentiation: Provide script templates; allow pairs instead of solo reports.

Colors in Our World.name: Color Nature Walk
Colors in Our World.overview: Students go on a 'color nature walk' around the school, observing and collecting colorful objects (e.g., blue flowers, green leaves), then create a color chart.
Colors in Our World.general_objective: Develop observation skills and color vocabulary through nature exploration.
specific_objectives: Collect and categorize natural objects by color
activities: Guided nature walk with collection bags

rubric_criteria.Participation: Active engagement in walk and sorting

Colors in Our World.technology_integration: Use a tablet to take photos of objects and create a digital color chart with the teacher's help.
Colors in Our World.differentiation: Provide color swatches for matching; limit color focus to 2-3 primary colors.

Colors in Our World.name: Color Sorting Game
Colors in Our World.overview: Students play a color sorting game matching colored objects (e.g., blue birds, red cars) to their corresponding colors on a chart.
Colors in Our World.general_objective: Reinforce color recognition through interactive play and matching activities.
specific_objectives: Use color vocabulary in game context
Follow game rules and take turns
activities: Create or prepare color game materials

rubric_criteria.Vocabulary: Use of color names during play

Colors in Our World.technology_integration: Create digital version of matching game with tablet.
Colors in Our World.differentiation: Reduce number of colors; provide physical objects instead of pictures.

Colors in Our World.name: Color Collage
Colors in Our World.overview: Students create a collage using colored paper or magazine cutouts, labeling objects with sentences like 'This is a blue bird.'
Colors in Our World.general_objective: Develop artistic expression while practicing descriptive language with colors.
specific_objectives: Use glue and cutting skills appropriately
Colors in Our World.technology_integration: Create digital collage using simple art apps.
Colors in Our World.differentiation: Pre-cut shapes; provide sentence frames; allow focus on fewer colors.

My Family and I.name: Family Portrait
My Family and I.overview: Students draw a family portrait, labeling each family member with sentences like 'This is my mom.' and 'He is my brother.'
My Family and I.general_objective: Develop family vocabulary and personal expression through art.
specific_objectives: Use possessive adjectives correctly (my, his, her)
Write descriptive labels for each person
My Family and I.technology_integration: Create digital family tree using a simple app with photos and names.
My Family and I.differentiation: Provide family member stickers; use sentence frames for labeling.

My Family and I.name: Family Member Interview
My Family and I.overview: Students ask a family member simple questions like 'Who is she/he?' and record their answers to share with the class.
My Family and I.general_objective: Develop interview skills and family relationship vocabulary.
My Family and I.technology_integration: Record audio interviews; create simple presentation slides.
My Family and I.differentiation: Provide pre-written questions; allow drawing instead of writing answers.

My Family and I.name: Digital Family Tree
My Family and I.overview: Students create a family tree using a digital family tree maker or a slide presentation app. They include simple sentences like 'This is my dad.' and answer questions such as 'Who is she?' with 'She is my grandmother.'
My Family and I.general_objective: Develop technology skills while exploring family relationships.
activities: Present and answer questions about tree
My Family and I.technology_integration: Use family tree apps or presentation software with teacher guidance.
My Family and I.differentiation: Provide paper-based alternative; use icons instead of photos.

Colors of Things That Go.name: Vehicle Color Matching
Colors of Things That Go.overview: Students create a matching game by drawing and coloring different vehicles (e.g., green cars and red buses) and matching them to color labels.
Colors of Things That Go.general_objective: Reinforce color and vehicle vocabulary through game creation.
specific_objectives: Identify common vehicles and their colors
Play game with correct color-vehicle matches
Use descriptive phrases in game context
Colors of Things That Go.technology_integration: Use a tablet to play a color and vehicle matching game or design one digitally.
Colors of Things That Go.differentiation: Provide vehicle templates; reduce number of matches.

Colors of Things That Go.name: Vehicle Drawing Game
Colors of Things That Go.overview: Students draw and color vehicles (cars, buses, bikes) and label them with sentences like 'This is a red bus.'
Colors of Things That Go.general_objective: Develop artistic skills while practicing descriptive language.
Colors of Things That Go.technology_integration: Use drawing apps on tablets for digital vehicle art.
Colors of Things That Go.differentiation: Provide tracing templates; use sentence frames.

Colors of Things That Go.name: Transportation Chart
Colors of Things That Go.overview: Students create a transportation chart with columns for vehicles and colors, labeling each with sentences like 'That is a blue car.'
Colors of Things That Go.general_objective: Develop organizational skills through chart creation and data representation.
specific_objectives: Categorize vehicles by type and color
Colors of Things That Go.technology_integration: Create digital chart using spreadsheet or presentation software.
Colors of Things That Go.differentiation: Provide pre-made chart template; work in small groups.

Shapes Around Us.name: Shape Collage
Shapes Around Us.overview: Children cut out pictures of objects in different shapes (circles, squares, rectangles) and create a collage, labeling each shape with sentences like 'This is a circle.'
Shapes Around Us.general_objective: Develop shape recognition through hands-on art creation.
specific_objectives: Find shape examples in real-world objects
Shapes Around Us.technology_integration: Use a digital collage-making tool or an interactive shape recognition app.
Shapes Around Us.differentiation: Provide pre-cut shapes; focus on 2-3 basic shapes.

Shapes Around Us.name: Shape Sorting Activity
Shapes Around Us.overview: Students sort shapes (e.g., squares, circles) into groups and describe each with simple sentences like 'This is a square.'
Shapes Around Us.general_objective: Develop classification skills and shape vocabulary.
Shapes Around Us.technology_integration: Use digital sorting games on tablets.
Shapes Around Us.differentiation: Reduce number of shape types; provide visual sorting guides.

Shapes Around Us.name: Digital Shape Game
Shapes Around Us.overview: Students play a digital shape recognition game, matching objects to their corresponding shapes and labeling them.
Shapes Around Us.general_objective: Develop technology skills while reinforcing shape recognition.
specific_objectives: Apply shape vocabulary in digital context
Shapes Around Us.technology_integration: Use interactive shape recognition apps and games.
Shapes Around Us.differentiation: Adjust game difficulty levels; provide physical shape references.

Shapes on Our Plates.name: Food Shape Match
Shapes on Our Plates.overview: Students collect pictures of different foods and match them to their shapes (e.g., round oranges, square crackers), creating a poster or chart.
Shapes on Our Plates.general_objective: Connect geometry to everyday life through food shapes.
Shapes on Our Plates.technology_integration: Use an app to take pictures of foods and match them to shapes digitally.
Shapes on Our Plates.differentiation: Provide pre-selected food pictures; focus on basic shapes.

Shapes on Our Plates.name: Food Shape Sorting
Shapes on Our Plates.overview: Students sort different foods into shape categories (e.g., round, square) and label them with simple sentences like 'This is a round orange.'
Shapes on Our Plates.general_objective: Develop classification skills using food items.
Shapes on Our Plates.differentiation: Provide shape templates for matching; reduce number of categories.

Shapes on Our Plates.name: Digital Food Shape Chart
Shapes on Our Plates.overview: Students create a digital food shape chart, labeling each food with sentences like 'The cracker is square.'
Shapes on Our Plates.general_objective: Develop digital creation skills while exploring food geometry.
specific_objectives: Use digital tools to create chart
activities: Collect and organize food shape data
Shapes on Our Plates.technology_integration: Use spreadsheet or presentation software for chart creation.
Shapes on Our Plates.differentiation: Provide chart template; work in pairs.

It's Our Garden.name: Garden Nature Journal
It's Our Garden.overview: Children explore the school garden, observe plants and trees, and record their findings in a nature journal, labeling each plant with simple sentences like 'This is a tall tree.'
It's Our Garden.general_objective: Develop observation and documentation skills in natural environment.
It's Our Garden.technology_integration: Use a nature journaling app to record findings and add digital photos.
It's Our Garden.differentiation: Provide journal templates; focus on 2-3 specific plants.

It's Our Garden.name: Garden Scavenger Hunt
It's Our Garden.overview: Students participate in a scavenger hunt in the school garden, finding objects like pink flowers and tall trees and labeling them with sentences.
It's Our Garden.general_objective: Develop exploration skills and descriptive language through game-based learning.
It's Our Garden.technology_integration: Use tablets for photo documentation during scavenger hunt.
It's Our Garden.differentiation: Simplify scavenger hunt list; provide visual clues.

It's Our Garden.name: Digital Nature Photo Book
It's Our Garden.overview: Students take photos of plants and trees in the school garden and create a digital photo book, labeling each plant with simple sentences like 'This is a pink flower.'
It's Our Garden.general_objective: Develop digital documentation skills while exploring nature.
It's Our Garden.technology_integration: Use photo book apps or presentation software for creation.
It's Our Garden.differentiation: Provide pre-taken photos; work in pairs on book creation.
